0.original: Shortly after World War II, Seedy got a job with the Customs Division of the U.S. Post Office Department. 2 00:00:07,120 --> 00:00:12,769 He liked his work, but after five years he became dissatisfied with the confinement, 3 00:00:12,800 --> 00:00:19,730 regular hours, low pay, and the seniority system with its relatively narrow chances for advancement. 4 00:00:20,160 --> 00:00:22,050 Then he got an idea. 5 00:00:22,480 --> 00:00:26,850 He had learned a great deal about what it takes to be a successful importer. 6 00:00:27,040 --> 00:00:33,090 Why not set himself up in the business of importing low-cost gift items and toys? 7 00:00:33,440 --> 00:00:40,210 C.D. knew many successful importers who didn't have his knowledge of the ins and outs of this business. 8 00:00:41,120 --> 00:00:46,850 It's been ten years now since C.D. decided he wanted to go into business for himself. 9 00:00:47,040 --> 00:00:50,690 But today, he's still working for the customs office. 10 00:00:50,960 --> 00:00:51,730 Why? 11 00:00:52,320 --> 00:00:56,850 Well, every time C.D. was just about ready to cut loose on his own, 12 00:00:57,040 --> 00:01:00,050 something happened that stopped him from taking action. 13 00:01:00,320 --> 00:01:07,570 Lack of money, economic recession, new baby, need for temporary security, trade restrictions, 14 00:01:07,680 --> 00:01:12,930 and more excuses all served as reasons for waiting, for postponing. 15 00:01:13,680 --> 00:01:18,690 The real truth is that C.D. let himself develop into a passivationist. 16 00:01:18,960 --> 00:01:22,610 He wanted conditions to be perfect before he took action. 17 00:01:22,720 --> 00:01:27,009 Since conditions were never perfect, C.D. never took action. 18 00:01:28,320 --> 00:01:36,289 Here are two things to do to help you avoid the costly mistake of waiting until conditions are perfect before you act.
0.b1: After World War II, Seedy worked for the U.S. Post Office Customs Division. He liked the job at first, but after five years, he felt unhappy because the work was boring, the pay was low, and he could not get a promotion easily. Then, he had an idea: he knew a lot about importing, so why not start his own business selling cheap gifts and toys? He knew many people who were successful importers even though they knew less than he did. It has been ten years since he first wanted to start his own business, but he still works at the customs office today. Why? Every time he was ready to quit, something stopped him. He used many excuses like a lack of money, a bad economy, a new baby, or trade rules to delay his plans. The truth is that he became a person who always waits. He wanted everything to be perfect before he started, but since things were never perfect, he never took action. Here are two ways to help you avoid the mistake of waiting for perfect conditions before you act.
0.cn: 二战结束后不久，希迪在美国邮政局海关部门找到了一份工作。起初他很喜欢这份工作，但五年后，他开始对这种受限的工作环境、固定的工作时间、微薄的薪水以及晋升机会渺茫的资历制度感到不满。后来，他产生了一个想法。他已经非常了解如何成为一名成功的进口商。为什么不自己创业，从事低成本礼品和玩具的进口生意呢？希迪认识许多成功的进口商，他们对这个行业的了解甚至还不如他。距离希迪决定自己创业已经过去十年了。但今天，他仍然在海关办公室工作。为什么？嗯，每当希迪准备好辞职单干时，总会发生一些事情阻止他采取行动。缺钱、经济衰退、添了新宝宝、需要暂时的稳定、贸易限制，以及更多的借口，都成了他等待和拖延的理由。事实是，希迪让自己变成了一个消极等待的人。他希望在采取行动之前一切条件都完美无缺。由于条件从未完美过，希迪也就从未采取过行动。这里有两件事可以帮助你避免那种“等到条件完美才行动”的代价高昂的错误。
0.ai_notes:
'World War II': a major global conflict from 1939 to 1945 that changed the world economy and politics.
'Customs Division': a government agency responsible for checking goods entering a country and collecting taxes on them.
'confinement': the feeling of being trapped or restricted in a small or limited space.
'seniority system': a policy where people get better pay or promotions based on how long they have worked at a company, rather than their talent.
'advancement': moving forward in one's career, such as getting a promotion or a higher-level job.
'importer': a person or company that brings goods from another country to sell in their own country.
'set himself up': to start one's own business or independent career.
'ins and outs': the detailed, practical facts or complicated parts of a specific situation or business.
'go into business for himself': to become self-employed or start a private company.
'cut loose': to leave a job or situation to become independent.
'taking action': doing something to achieve a goal instead of just thinking about it.
'economic recession': a period when a country's economy is weak and businesses are struggling.
'postponing': delaying something or putting it off until a later time.
'passivationist': a person who avoids taking action and waits for others or circumstances to change things for them.
'costly mistake': an error that results in a significant loss of time, money, or opportunity.
0.remarks:
'World War II': 1939年至1945年间改变世界经济和政治格局的一场重大全球冲突。
'Customs Division': 负责检查入境货物并征收税款的政府机构。
'confinement': 在狭小或有限空间内感到被困或受限的感觉。
'seniority system': 根据工作年限而非个人才能来决定薪资或晋升的制度。
'advancement': 在职业生涯中向前迈进，例如获得晋升或担任更高级别的职位。
'importer': 将外国商品带入本国销售的个人或公司。
'set himself up': 创办自己的企业或开启独立职业生涯。
'ins and outs': 特定情况或业务中详细的实际情况或复杂细节。
'go into business for himself': 成为自雇人士或创办私人公司。
'cut loose': 离开某份工作或某种境况以获得独立。
'taking action': 为实现目标而采取行动，而非仅仅空想。
'economic recession': 国家经济疲软、企业经营困难的时期。
'postponing': 推迟某事或将其延后到以后处理。
'passivationist': 避免采取行动，等待他人或环境改变现状的人。
'costly mistake': 导致时间、金钱或机会重大损失的错误。
